greetings: Well met, traveler. What brings you this way?
Ah, a fresh face. Welcome.
Good day to you. Don't see many strangers around here.
Hail, friend. You look like you've been on the road a while.
Welcome. Always good to see new folk pass through.
You've picked an interesting time to visit, I'll say that much.
Ho there. Come to trade, or just passing through?
Greetings. Make yourself comfortable, such as it is.
A visitor! That's a rare thing in these parts.
Well now, you don't look like trouble. That's a good start.
Afternoon. Or is it morning? Hard to keep track some days.
Come in, come in. No need to stand on ceremony here.
Didn't expect company today. But you're welcome all the same.
Well, hello there. You're a long way from anywhere, aren't you?
Another traveler. The road must be busy this season.
New in town? Or new to me, at least.
The road finally brought someone worth talking to. Welcome.
State your business, friend. Or don't. I'm just being polite.
You've got the look of someone who's been walking too long. Rest a moment.
Can I help you? I mean that genuinely, by the way.
Well, would you look at that. A new face around here.
You look like you could use a warm meal and a dry seat. Welcome.
Greetings, traveler. What news from the road?
A stranger! We don't get many. What brings you to our little corner?
Hail and well met. Mind the step on the way in.
Good to see someone new. Gets quiet around here without visitors.
You're braver than most, coming this way. Or more lost. Either way, welcome.
I was starting to think nobody new would pass through again. Glad to be wrong.
The gate hasn't seen a new face in weeks. You'll turn heads.
Come closer, let me get a look at you. Ah, a traveler. Welcome.
Step in from the cold. Or the heat. Whatever the weather is doing today.
A visitor from beyond the ridge. That's noteworthy.
You've got road dust on you. Must have come a fair way.
Don't mind the noise. It's always like this around here. Welcome.
I hope the road treated you kindly. You're safe enough here.
Fresh company! I was starting to go stale talking to the same three people.
You must be the traveler they mentioned at the gate. Come, sit.
I'll admit, I wasn't expecting anyone today. But here you are.
Welcome to our settlement. Small as it is, it's home.
Careful where you step. The ground around here has ideas of its own.
A newcomer! That's either very good or very bad. Let's find out which.
Well, the road brings all sorts. I'd say you're one of the better sorts.
Haven't seen your face before. That makes you interesting by default.
You picked a curious time to arrive, but we'll make the best of it.
You look like you've seen a few things on that road. Pull up a seat.
Hello, stranger. Don't worry, that title won't last long around here.
You've made it this far. That alone says something about you.
Traveler, is it? We could use some fresh perspective. Welcome.
I notice you're not from around here. That's perfectly fine.
Good day. I'd offer you something to drink, but I haven't got much.
You're the first new person I've seen in a tenday. Let me savor this.
Welcome. If you need anything, just speak up. We're not formal here.
The patrol mentioned someone coming up the road. That must be you.
Greetings. You've arrived at an interesting moment in our little story.
I see you found us. Not everyone does, tucked away as we are.
Ho, traveler. The road out here isn't for the faint of heart.
Good timing. You've arrived just before the rain. Or after it. Hard to tell.
A visitor! And one who looks like they can handle themselves. Welcome.
Step right in. We're not much, but we're honest folk.
Glad to see someone still travels these roads. We were beginning to wonder.
Hello, hello. Don't be shy. We're friendlier than we look.
You've got that wide-eyed look travelers get when they first see this place.
Welcome. Take a moment. Catch your breath. Then we'll talk.
A face I don't recognize. That's the best kind of surprise around here.
The wind blew you our way, did it? Well, welcome all the same.
New to the area, or just passing through? Either way, glad you're here.
You've come at a time when we could use some outside company.
I noticed you at the gate. Thought I'd introduce myself.
Well then. Here you are, and here I am. Welcome, for what it's worth.
The road in is worse than it looks, isn't it? Glad you made it.
We don't bite. Well, most of us don't. Welcome.
Good day, traveler. I hope our little settlement meets your expectations.
You look like someone with a story. I look like someone who wants to hear it.
Let me guess: you didn't come all this way for the scenery.
Fresh boots on old ground. That's always a welcome sight.
Welcome, stranger. You're among friends here, if you'll have us.
The fact that you're here tells me you're either brave or desperate. Welcome either way.
A visitor from beyond the valley. That's cause for a smile.
Good to meet you. I'd shake your hand, but mine are filthy. Welcome anyway.
You've got the bearing of someone who's been places. Come, tell me about them.
Ah, a new arrival. Let me be the first to welcome you properly.
Not every day we see a fresh face. Make yourself at home.
You look like the road has stories to tell, and you've heard most of them.
Traveler, welcome. I hope our hospitality makes up for our remoteness.
I've been expecting someone. Didn't know it would be you, but here we are.
Come, come. Don't stand in the doorway. That's a spot for drafts, not guests.
Another soul brave enough to venture out this way. You have my respect.
You must be tired. Rest here a while. We'll talk when you're ready.
Hail, friend. I use that word loosely, but I mean it sincerely.
Good day. Or good evening. Or good whatever it is by the time you got here.
Welcome to the edge of the known world. Or close enough.
I'd offer you the grand tour, but you can see most of it from where you're standing.
Well, you're here. That's half the battle. The other half is figuring out why.
You have an honest look about you. That's rare enough to mention.
I see we've got company. The good kind, I hope.
Welcome. Fair warning: once people start talking to you here, they don't stop.
Stranger on the road. That used to mean trouble. Now it just means company.
If you've come looking for something, you might just find it here.
I don't get out much, so I appreciate when the world comes to me. Welcome.
Step carefully: the path is rougher than it looks. But you're welcome here.
A visitor! Let me dust off my manners. It's been a while.
From the look of you, you've had quite the journey. Sit down, rest.
We're a quiet settlement. You might be the most exciting thing that happens this week.
Good to see someone new. Names and stories can come later. Just welcome.
Well met on the road. Though I suppose this isn't quite the road anymore.
You've got a traveler's eye. Always looking, always taking things in. I respect that.
Greetings, friend. I assume you've come here on purpose. Not everyone does.
Welcome. Don't let the quiet fool you. Plenty happens beneath the surface.
Not many make it out this far. You must have your reasons.
Well, come on then. I won't keep you standing in the sun.
A familiar road, a new face. The two best things a day can bring.
If you're here, you're welcome. That's our policy.
Hello, traveler. I'll admit, it's a relief to see someone who isn't a local.
The path here is long, but you made it. That counts for something.
Welcome. I'll answer what I can, if you've got questions.
You look like someone who didn't just wander in by accident.
Good day to you. If you need direction, I know these roads well enough.
A new face in the crowd. Or what passes for a crowd around here.
You don't look like you're from around here. That's not a complaint.
Another pair of boots on the road. Hope they've got some miles left in them.
I was just about to close up, but I suppose you're worth staying open for.
You walked all the way here? On purpose? Well, I'm flattered.
Haven't had a visitor in so long I almost forgot how to say hello. Hello.
Right then, stranger. Spit it out or sit down. Either works for me.
You've got that look. The one where you want something but don't know what yet.
The last traveler who came through here had nothing interesting to say. You better.
I was about to take a break, but conversations count as breaks. Welcome.
You look less confused than most who end up here. That's promising.
Keep your coin in your pocket for now. We talk first around here.
If you're selling something, I'm not buying. If you're buying something, let's talk.
Hmm. You don't have the look of a thief. That's the second-best first impression.
You look like you slept rough last night. No judgment: I've been there.
Well, you made it past the goats. That's the real test around here.
I'd wave, but my hands are full. Consider this a verbal wave.
You're here. I'm here. Might as well make the most of it.
I can tell you're not local by the way you're looking at everything.
Slow day. Slower now that you've shown up. That's not an insult, I promise.
Whatever you've heard about this place, it's probably half true.
Don't take this the wrong way, but I wasn't expecting you. Or anyone, really.
You look like you've got questions. I might have answers. No guarantees.
I've seen that road wear down better folk than you. Glad you made it.
Come in out of the wind. It'll steal the words right out of your mouth.
You're new. I know because I know everyone, and I don't know you.
Right, a stranger. Give me a moment to remember how to be hospitable.
If the road brought you here, it had a reason. Whether you know it or not.
You've stumbled onto something decent here. Small, but decent.
That path you took in? It used to be better. Everything used to be better.
You look steady on your feet. That's more than I can say for the last one who came through.
I don't usually talk to strangers. But you've got a face that invites it.
The sun's not getting any higher. Come on, step inside.
I hope you like plain food and plainer conversation. That's what we've got.
First time here? You'll figure out the layout quick. There's not much of it.
Look at you. Clean clothes, all your teeth. You must be from somewhere nice.
I've been watching you walk up the road for the last ten minutes. Thought you'd never get here.
You're the first stranger I've seen since the rains stopped. Welcome.
Keep your voice down. Not for any reason, I just like the quiet.
Well, you've found the edge of nowhere. Congratulations.
You look like the kind of person who has opinions about things. Pull up a chair.
I'd say this is a surprise, but surprises imply I was paying attention. Welcome.
Watch your head coming through. The doorframe's been like that for years.
Just so we're clear: I'm friendly, not helpful. There's a difference.
Whatever you're after, I hope it's patience. We've got plenty of that here.
You walked right past the sign that says no visitors. Lucky for you I can't read either.
Good, someone new to complain to. Welcome.
I can see from here you've got a story. Don't worry, I'm not going to ask for it.
The chickens don't usually scatter for strangers. You must walk heavy.
Travelers used to come through here every week. Now it's been months. You're overdue.
Mind the puddle. Mind the dog. Mind the gap in the fence. Welcome.
I don't have much, but I've got time. And you look like you need someone to listen.
You picked the least interesting day to show up. Tomorrow would've been better.
You've got road grit in your hair. Don't worry about it: so does everyone else.
I'll be honest, I'm too tired to be impressed by a traveler. But welcome.
Good. A person. I was starting to think I'd have to talk to myself again.
You showed up right when I ran out of things to do. That's either lucky or suspicious.
Come in. Ignore the mess. It's organized, I just can't explain how.
I'd offer you the good seat, but there's only one seat and it's mine. Stand for a bit?
Something tells me you're going to be more interesting than my morning has been.
You look like someone who knows how to listen. That's worth more than gold out here.
This is the part where I say welcome and you tell me what you want. Go ahead.
You've got an outsider's posture. All tense. Relax: nothing here bites. Mostly.
I'd introduce myself properly, but I'm in the middle of something. Give me a second.
The road here doesn't forgive mistakes. You must have made very few.
You smell like pine and campfire. I approve of both.
New face, same old settlement. I hope we don't bore you.
I'll save us both some time: yes, it's always this quiet.
You walked through the front gate like you owned the place. I like that.
I see the road didn't break you. That's a good sign.
Hmm. You're not what I expected. Then again, I wasn't expecting anyone.
You look like you've eaten today. That puts you ahead of half the people here.
I notice you haven't asked for anything yet. That's refreshing.
Welcome. The well's over there, the outhouse is over there. Everything else, ask.
Another wanderer looking for something. Well, come on: I'll see what I've got.
You've got honest eyes. Or very good dishonest eyes. Either way, welcome.
I'd make a fuss about a visitor, but I'm not the fuss-making type.
If you've come to gawk at the settlement, there's not much to gawk at. If you've come to talk, pull up a chair.
You look capable. That's not always the case with the people who end up here.
I hope you're not in a hurry. Nothing happens fast out this way.
You've arrived. Don't expect a fanfare: the trumpet player left years ago.
I'm going to go ahead and assume you're not here to cause trouble.
New boots or old boots, everyone's welcome. Come in.
I see you looking around. It's not much, but we're proud of it.
You've crossed a lot of ground to get here. I can see it in your legs.
Easy now, no need to rush. The day's long and so is the conversation.
I was about to make something to eat. You want in? Don't answer yet, just come sit.
You've shown up just as the light's getting good. Nice timing.
I don't hand out trust easily, but I don't withhold it without reason either. Welcome.
I was talking to the wall before you showed up. You're already an improvement.
You've got a bit of the wild on you. The road does that.
I'm guessing you didn't see the shortcut. Nobody ever does.
You're standing in my light. But that's all right: I'll move.
I won't ask where you're from. Around here, it's more polite to ask where you're going.
Well, there's a person I've never seen before. That's my whole review of you so far.
The ground is dry and the sky is clear. A good day to meet someone new.
If you're looking for excitement, you've come to the wrong place. But if you want peace, you might be exactly right.
Go ahead and set your pack down. Nobody's going to take it.
You got here the hard way, I bet. There is no easy way, but I like to think there is.
Someone told me a stranger was asking for directions to this place. That's flattering.
I'm not good with first impressions, but I warm up. Give me a minute.
You've picked a fine day to arrive. Rain yesterday, rain tomorrow. Dry right now.
I haven't talked to anyone new in weeks. Forgive me if I say too much.
You look competent. I hope that's accurate and not just good posture.
The dogs didn't bark at you. They bark at everyone. I'm not sure what that means.
This is a working settlement, so don't mind the noise and the dirt. Welcome.
Your timing is something. I just finished what I was doing and had nothing to do next.
I can tell by the way you walked in that you've done this before. Visited settlements, I mean.
You look like you've got a purpose. I respect that, even if I don't know what it is.
I hope the road signs made sense. I wrote half of them, and I've been told my handwriting is terrible.
There's water in the barrel if you need it. There's conversation at this table if you want it.
You made it. Wasn't sure you would, looking at those clouds earlier.
You look like someone who doesn't ask for help unless they need it. I can work with that.
Alright, you're here. Take a breath. You look like you haven't taken one in miles.
I'm no good at welcomes, so I'll just nod and point you toward the shade.
You don't seem like the type to make trouble. But then again, the type never does.
Come out of the sun. You're turning red and we haven't even spoken yet.
A stranger who looks like they know what they're doing. That's new around here.
I'd ask how the road was, but I can see it on your face.
One more person in this settlement and we can call ourselves a town. Welcome, you might be that person.
I won't pretend this is the best place you've ever been. But it's not the worst either.
You'll find we're straightforward folk here. I'll start: what can I do for you?
The roof leaks, the floor creaks, and the fire smokes. But the company's good. Welcome.
You arrived quietly. I appreciate that. The last visitor came in hollering.
I'd give you directions, but there's only one road and you're already on it.
If you're waiting for a warm reception, this is about as warm as it gets.
Something about you says you've traveled with a purpose. Good. Purposeless travelers are exhausting.
I was in the middle of arguing with myself. You've saved me from losing.
You've got the right amount of caution in your eyes. Not too much, not too little.
Let me guess: someone told you to come here, and you're not entirely sure why.
You don't look like the settlers we usually get. That's probably a good thing.
I notice you knocked. Most people just walk in. I appreciate the manners.
Not to be blunt, but you look like you could sit down for about three days. Take it easy.
Good, you're here before dark. The trail gets tricky after sunset.
This is the part where I pretend I'm busy and you pretend you're not tired. Or we skip all that.
You've found the one person in this settlement who enjoys talking to strangers. Lucky you.
Don't just stand there blinking at me. Come in or move along.
Oh, a traveler. I thought you were somebody I owed money to. Welcome.
The road out here eats people. The fact that you're standing means something.
You look like you've been arguing with the weather. The weather won.
I see you eyeing the settlement. It's not much, but it works.
I'd stand up to greet you properly, but my knees have other plans.
Another soul on the road. I was beginning to think the world had emptied out.
You've got the build of someone who does things with their hands. I respect that.
Welcome. The last person who came through here left in a hurry. I hope you're different.
I can see you've got your own way of doing things. That's fine: so do I.
You found us. That's either skill or luck, and I'll give you credit for both.
I was having a perfectly unremarkable day until you showed up. Thank you.
Stranger, huh? We'll fix that quick enough.
You've got dirt on your face. No, the other side. Never mind: welcome.
Slow down. Nobody's chasing you here. At least, I hope not.
I notice you checked over your shoulder before coming in. Smart.
You look like someone who actually wants to talk. That's rarer than you think.
First visitor in a while. I should probably clean up. Too late now.
You walked straight up to me. Most newcomers hover by the gate for an hour first.
I'll be straight with you: I've got nothing to sell and nothing to hide. Welcome.
You carry yourself well for someone who's been on the road that long.
Good, you look like someone who won't talk my ear off. Unless you will. Either way, hello.
I was just thinking this place needed a fresh pair of eyes. Here you are.
You look lost, but in the good way. The curious way.
I'm the wrong person to give you directions, but the right person to give you a welcome.
Don't worry about the staring. People here just aren't used to new faces.
You've got a calm about you. That's appreciated around here.
Welcome. If anyone gives you trouble, they'll hear about it from me.
You picked the right door to walk through. Or the only door. Same thing, really.
I've seen all kinds come through here. You don't fit any of the usual molds. That's good.
New faces make old places feel different. Welcome.
If you're looking for somewhere to belong, you could do worse than here.
You've come a long way for what might be a short conversation. Let's make it count.
You've still got your pack on. Set it down: you're not leaving yet.
I was starting to think the road had closed down. Glad to see it hasn't.
The air's better here than wherever you came from. I can tell by the way you're breathing.
Good. A real person. I've been talking to the cat all morning.
Most people who come here are running from something. You look like you're running toward something.
Welcome. Don't let the lack of fanfare fool you: we're glad you're here.
You've got that look people get when they realize this place is smaller than they expected.
I don't ask questions on the first meeting. That's a second meeting privilege.
Well, someone finally had the nerve to come up and say hello. Or did I say it first?
You don't owe me an explanation for being here. But I'll listen to one if you've got it.
I can tell you're sizing this place up. Take your time. It doesn't change.
Welcome to the middle of nowhere. Population: a few more, now that you're here.
You look like you could stand to sit down for a good long while.
I'd give you the whole welcome speech, but I forgot it years ago. Just come in.
You made it through the gap in the hills. Not everyone notices it.
I'm going to guess you're hungry. Everyone who arrives here is hungry.
The sun's in my eyes, so I can't see you well. But welcome anyway.
Another face for the record books. We don't keep record books, but the thought counts.
You've arrived at a decent hour. The last traveler showed up at midnight.
You haven't said anything yet and I already like you. That's a low bar, but still.
I'll be honest: most of the people here are terrible conversationalists. You look promising.
You've got road-hardened hands. Whatever you do, you've been doing it a while.
I see you looking at the buildings. They lean, I know. They've always leaned.
Welcome. If you hear shouting later, don't worry: that's just how we talk around here.
Come closer. I don't bite. The dog might, though. Just kidding. Mostly.
I was in the middle of nothing important. You're a welcome interruption.
You showed up at a good time. I just finished being grumpy about something.
I'll tell you the same thing I tell everyone: behave yourself and you'll be fine.
From one tired person to what looks like another: welcome.
I've been staring at that horizon for days. Nice to look at a person for a change.
Well, you're not armed to the teeth. That's a better entrance than usual.
You look like someone I could trust with a small favor. Maybe later.
I don't know your story yet, and that's the best part. Welcome.
Hey there, stranger. Come on in.
Oh, someone new! What a treat.
You look like you could use a friendly face. Here I am.
The door's open and so am I. Welcome.
Come right in. We were just saying how it'd been too long since we had a visitor.
Well, hello. You look like you belong here already.
A traveler! I love meeting new folk.
I hope you're staying a while. We could use the company.
Welcome. You got here just when things were getting dull.
You look like good people. Come sit with me.
Hey, glad you stopped by. Most people walk right past us.
Come in, warm up. Or cool down. Whichever you need.
A friendly face on the road is always worth stopping for.
You're welcome here, no questions asked.
I could tell from the road you were headed this way. Glad you made it.
Take a load off. You've earned it just by getting here.
Another traveler! The more the merrier, I say.
You look like someone I'd like to know. Welcome.
Hey now, we've got a visitor! Come on in.
Pull up something to sit on. I'm happy to have the company.
Well, aren't you a welcome sight.
You look friendly enough. I'll take my chances. Welcome.
New face, warm welcome. That's how we do things here.
Good to see you. And I do mean that.
You picked the right settlement to wander into.
I was hoping someone interesting would come through today.
You've got a kind look about you. That goes a long way out here.
The more people who pass through, the better. Come in.
A visitor who doesn't look like trouble. What a nice change.
You're here, and that's reason enough to smile.
Hey. What's going on.
Oh, hi. Didn't see you there for a second.
Yeah, come in. Make yourself at home.
Hey, how's it going? New around here, right?
Oh, a new one. All right, welcome I guess.
Hey. You lost, or did you mean to come here?
What's up. You need something, or just passing through?
Oh, someone new. Cool.
You look like you know where you're going. That's more than I can say most days.
Hey, how are you. Just come in, it's fine.
Huh, a stranger. Don't get many of those.
Oh, hey. Yeah, come in. Door's open.
You look all right. Come on in.
Passing through? Staying a bit? Either way.
Hey. Grab a seat if you want.
New face. Good enough for me. Welcome.
Sure, come in. I'm not doing anything important anyway.
Oh, hello. Yeah, this is the place. Whatever place you were looking for.
You're new, right? I'd remember that face.
Hey there. Don't mind the mess.
What brings you out this way? Just curious.
Nice day for a walk, huh? You must have been doing a lot of it.
Hey, no need to hover. Just come in.
Looks like the road brought someone interesting for once.
Oh good, a person. I was running low on those.
You look like you got here the normal way. Good, that's easier.
Well, hey. You seem normal enough.
Come on in, I'm just sitting here anyway.
Another day, another stranger. Nothing wrong with that.
Ah, you found us. Some people walk right by without noticing.
What do you want.
Yeah? Who are you.
Great, a stranger. Just what I needed today.
State your business or keep walking.
I don't have all day. What is it.
You look lost. Are you lost.
What brings you here. And make it quick.
Another one. Fine, come in.
Don't touch anything. What do you need.
I'm busy. But talk if you must.
Spit it out, stranger. What do you want.
I've seen better-looking travelers. But you're here, so welcome.
Don't expect any special treatment. You get what everyone gets.
You're interrupting my peace and quiet. This better be worth it.
Let me guess: you want something.
Another wanderer looking for a handout. Or are you different?
I hope you're not expecting a warm reception. This is as warm as it gets.
Yeah, I see you. What of it.
Keep your hands where I can see them and we'll get along fine.
You look like you're about to ask me a question. Go ahead.
I was having a perfectly good day. But all right, hello.
If you're selling something, save your breath.
What, never seen someone minding their own business before?
I suppose you want to talk. Everyone wants to talk.
You look like effort. I'm low on effort today.
Another person with needs. Fine. What is it.
Don't stand there gawking. Either come in or don't.
Yeah, hi. I'm thrilled.
I'm not in the mood for small talk. But I'm not in the mood for rudeness either. So, hello.
You woke me up. I mean, I was awake, but still.
What are you looking at. Never seen a settlement before?
Hold on. I don't know you.
Stop right there. Who sent you.
I've got my eye on you, stranger.
Don't come any closer until I know who you are.
The last stranger who came through here caused nothing but headaches.
You're not from around here. That much is obvious.
I'll be watching you. Nothing personal.
What's your business here. And don't be vague about it.
We've had trouble with outsiders before. Don't add to it.
I don't trust easy, and I don't plan to start now.
You look harmless enough. But looks lie.
Who are you and why should I care.
I'll give you a chance. One chance.
Don't try anything funny. We look after our own here.
I'm not unfriendly. I'm just careful.
You've got a strange look about you. What's your story.
I'm going to ask you to keep your distance for now.
Most newcomers come with baggage. What's yours.
You'll have to earn trust around here. We don't give it away.
I've learned the hard way: strangers mean complications.
You don't look dangerous. But then again, the dangerous ones never do.
I hope you're not planning on sticking around. We've got enough problems.
Something about you puts me on edge. Can't say what.
I'll be polite, but I won't be careless. Welcome, I suppose.
Don't mistake my hospitality for trust.
You're the second stranger this month. That's unusual and I don't like unusual.
I've got half a mind to send you back the way you came.
We get all sorts out here. You'd better be the good sort.
I don't know what you want, but I know what I want: no trouble.
Keep your hands to yourself and we'll be fine.
Last time someone new showed up, things went sideways. Just saying.
Oh, hello. Sorry, long day.
You're here. Good, I think. I'm too tired to care either way.
Welcome, I suppose. Forgive me, I haven't slept much.
Another person. I barely have energy for the ones I know.
Come in if you want. I don't have the energy to stop you.
Hello. Excuse the yawn. It's not you.
I'd greet you properly, but I've been on my feet since dawn.
A visitor. That's nice. I think. Give me a moment.
Come in, sit down. At least one of us should be resting.
You look tired. That makes two of us.
Welcome. I'd stand up, but I just sat down for the first time today.
The day's been long and it's only half over. But welcome.
I'm running on fumes. But I've got enough left to say hello.
Sure, come in. I was just trying to remember what sleep feels like.

You've traveled light. I admire that. Most of us can't let go of our stuff.
The road curves right before you see us. Catches everyone off guard.
I see you've been eating on the move. I can always tell.
You're here and you're whole. That's the important thing.
I've been fixing the same hinge for an hour. I'm thrilled to do literally anything else. Welcome.
You've got good timing: the bread just came out of the oven.
The settlement looks better from inside. Trust me.
I notice you stepped around the loose plank. Someone's been paying attention.
You came in through the old gate. The new one's around back. Nobody uses the new one.
I was debating whether to start a new project or take a nap. You've given me a third option.
Welcome. The sunset from here is worth the trip alone.
You don't look like you need rescuing. That's refreshing.
I was just about to wander off and do nothing. Glad I didn't.
The chickens seem to like you. I'll use that as a character reference.
You came from the direction nobody comes from. Interesting.
I was thinking the world had forgotten about us. Then here you are.
The stone steps are slippery. You made it up in one piece. Good.
I just swept. For the first time in a week. Your timing is something else.
You look like you appreciate quiet places. This is the quietest.
Welcome to the one spot on the road where the view's worth stopping for.
I see you noticed the flowers. I planted those. They almost died twice.
You don't look like you're running from anything. That's nice.
The air up here is cleaner. You'll notice it after a minute.
I haven't seen a traveler since the bridge washed out. Did they fix it?
You arrived just in time for the most boring part of the day. Welcome anyway.
Careful of the step there. Good. You made it. Welcome.
You've got the eyes of someone who notices things. I like that.
I was trying to remember the last time someone came through. It's been a while.
You look like you could use some shade and some conversation.
The birds went quiet when you walked up. They're cautious too.
I was talking to the plants. You're an upgrade.
You made it before the trail got dark. Smart timing.
I can tell by your stride you've been walking since morning.
You found the one settlement that doesn't advertise. We're exclusive. By accident.
I'm going to guess: you came from the lowlands. The elevation change shows on people.
The old bridge held for you? Good. I wasn't sure it would.
You're standing in the best light. It makes you look heroic. Just a little.
Welcome. I'd clean up, but this is as clean as it gets.
You walked in like you belonged. I'm not going to argue.
The wind shifted right before you arrived. Coincidence, probably.
I notice you're looking at the buildings. They're sturdier than they seem.
You came during the quiet hours. The loud hours are worse. Trust me.
There's soot in the air from the forge. Sorry about that.
You've got road in your boots and stories in your eyes. Welcome.
I keep a seat open for visitors. It doesn't get used much. But today it does.
You came the long way. I can tell because there is no short way.
I was watching a beetle crawl across the wall when you showed up. You're much more interesting.
The settlement doesn't look like much from the outside. It doesn't look like much from the inside either. But it's good.
You walked up so naturally I almost thought you lived here.
I'm guessing this isn't the destination. It's a stop along the way. That's fine too.
You came alone, on foot, in daylight. You're either brave or sensible. Maybe both.
I've been sitting in the same spot all day. You've just traveled who knows how far. I'm a little jealous.
The way you held the gate tells me you've been around places like this before.
You've got the patience of someone who walks a lot. I respect it.
The creek was running high last I checked. Did it give you trouble?
I notice you looked up at the sky before coming in. Expecting rain?
You've got a traveler's tan. It's distinctive.
I'm guessing you didn't eat breakfast. I know that look.
You found us at the end of the road. There's something poetic about that.
The dust you kicked up on the way in is still settling. That's how recent you are.
You've got a calm voice. I could tell even before you spoke. It's in the posture.
I've been wondering who'd come next. Now I know.
Welcome to the place where the road decides to stop.
You've crossed more ground today than I'll cross all week.
I was out of things to think about. Now I've got something.
The valley below is louder. Up here, it's just wind and footsteps. Welcome.
You picked the right season to visit. Winter's another story.
I'd bet you didn't expect to find anything up this way.
The well water's cold. The fire's warm. Between the two, you'll be comfortable.
You don't have the look of someone who complains. Thank goodness.
I was just thinking this place could use some new energy. And here you are, all full of it.
You took the high road. Literally. The low road floods.
Welcome. The only rule here is be decent. You look like you've got that covered.
You've traveled far enough to appreciate something simple. That's what we've got.
You walked through the wildflowers without stepping on any. I noticed.
Well, the sun's out and so are you. Good day for both.
I was counting clouds. You're more interesting. Barely.
You've got the right kind of tired on you. The earned kind.
The settlement dog hasn't barked. That means you passed the vibe check.
I see you came through the pine grove. That's the scenic route.
You've got sap on your sleeve. The trees got friendly on the way in, I see.
I was running out of reasons to sit outside. You just gave me one.
The cobblestones are uneven. You navigated them well.
I see the trail markers held up. I repainted them last month.
You're about the fifth person to walk through that gate this season. But who's counting.
You look like you slept under a decent tree last night. There are a few good ones out that way.
The morning light hits this place just right. I'm glad you're seeing it.
You've come at a time when the gardens are full. Lucky timing.
I was about to argue with a fence post. You're better company. Slightly.
The last time the gate opened like that, it was the wind. This time it's a person. Better.
I see you're carrying something wrapped up carefully. I won't ask. Welcome.
You got past the brambles. That's basically an entrance exam around here.
The air changed when you walked in. Warmer, somehow.
You came at midday. The shadows are shortest and the welcome is longest.
I notice things. Like the fact that you walked in without hesitation.
You found the one path that doesn't loop back on itself. Congratulations.
The bench out front was built for moments like this. Sit.
I was just saying to nobody in particular that we needed fresh perspective.
You've got trail dirt in layers. That's commitment.
Welcome. I'd tell you the rules, but there aren't many.
You came from the direction of the old mill. Is it still standing?
I was watching the shadows move. You moved faster. Welcome.
You got here before the afternoon heat. You know what you're doing.
The gate latch is tricky. The fact that you got in means you're patient.
Welcome. Take the first deep breath you've taken all day.
I notice you're not asking for anything yet. Just taking it in. Good instinct.
The breeze brought you in. Or the road. Or luck. Welcome either way.
You look like someone who'd sit by a fire and just listen. We need that.
I've lived here every day and you've been here five seconds. But I can tell you see it differently.
You walked in during the best hour. The light's gentle and so are the people.
The stone wall catches the last light of afternoon. I see you noticed.
You've found the place where the noise of the world gets a little quieter.
Welcome. I hope you find whatever it is you didn't know you were looking for.
The gate's always open. The welcome is, too.
I see you looking at the horizon. It's different from this side, isn't it?
You arrived. The settlement just got one person richer.
I was running out of daylight and things to say. You fixed both.
The road that led you here is the same one I've been watching all morning.
You came in gently. I appreciate that more than you know.
Well, here you are. And here I am. Let's see what comes of it.
The trail was kind to you, from the look of it. Welcome to where it ends.
I notice you paused at the gate. Taking it all in? Good. There's more inside.
You picked the only door that works. The other two are decorative.
I see you've got a good pair of walking legs. Welcome.
The dust cloud behind you was impressive. Welcome.
I was debating lunch when you showed up. Debate's over: talking instead.
You've got the look of someone who's eaten trail rations for too long.
I see you wiped your boots before coming in. Manners. I like it.
You came from the ridge side. That's the steep way.
I was watching the clouds when something more interesting appeared. You.
The settlement's small, but the welcome's big. Come in.
You've got the gait of someone who's walked a hundred roads.
The lantern's still lit. That means we're open for visitors.
I see you paused at the tree. Everyone does. It's a good tree.
You don't look like you need directions, but I'll offer them anyway.
I was about to yell at the crows. You're much better company.
The trail's been quiet all week. You're the first interesting sound.
You walked in like you'd been here in a dream. Welcome to the real version.
I see you brought the sunset with you. Good entrance.
The door squeaks. You came in anyway. I respect the commitment.
Most people hesitate at the threshold. You just stepped right through.
You've got the look of someone who carries their weight well.
The settlement's been peaceful. Your arrival doesn't change that.
I was talking to the fire. You're warmer company.
You came in at the golden hour. Everything looks kinder right now.
I notice you looked around before speaking. Smart and patient.
You've got trail dust in your eyebrows. Just so you know.
The path from the east is rocky. I can see it in your walk.
You came through the woods, didn't you? The pine needles gave you away.
I see you noticed the old wheel by the gate. Nobody knows why it's there.
You look like you could use less walking and more sitting.
I was running out of daylight and motivation. Your arrival fixes one of those.
The rooster crowed when you walked in. I choose to believe that's a greeting.
You look like someone who sleeps light and moves quick.
I was counting the stones in the wall. You're more interesting than stones.
The path loops back three times before it gets here. You made it through the loops.
I noticed you came the same way the merchants used to come. Years ago.
You've got the stance of someone who trusts their own feet.
I was having a staring contest with a bird. I lost. But now I've got a visitor.
The settlement's not on the way to anything. You came here on purpose.
I see you carrying yourself like someone who's made peace with the road.
You walked in during the only quiet hour. Good instinct.
The wind picked up right as you arrived. Dramatic entrance.
I was about to give up on the day. You saved it.
You've got the demeanor of someone who thinks before they speak. I appreciate that.
The last footprints on that trail were mine. Now yours are there too.
You came in without a sound. I like the quiet ones.
I see you've been walking with the sun at your back. Smart.
The rain held off just long enough for you to get here.
You look like someone who'd share their last piece of bread.
I notice you stopped to pet the cat. Good sign.
You came in with the breeze. Light and easy.
The settlement hasn't had a visitor since the road dried out.
You've got a way of standing that says you're listening even when you're not talking.
I was about to have a very unproductive afternoon. You changed that.
You came from the direction of the old watchtower. Is it still there?
The path gets prettier the last mile in. I hope you noticed.
I see you're taking your time. That's the right approach here.
You walked past the broken cart without asking about it. I appreciate that.
The smoke from the chimney probably led you here. That's by design.
You've got the face of someone who's made good decisions today.
I was going to spend the afternoon alone. Plans change. Welcome.
You came in through the gap in the hedge. Most people don't see it.
The sun's hitting the settlement just right. Glad you caught it.
You look like the kind of person who'd remember this place.
I was about to close for the day. But I'll stay open for you.
You've got the rhythm of someone who knows how to pace themselves.
returnGreetings: Back again? Good to see a familiar face.
You've returned! I was wondering if you would.
Ah, you again. Settling in, are you?
Welcome back. The road treats you well, I hope.
I remember you. Glad you found your way back.
Returned so soon? Must be something keeping you around.
Well, well. Our paths cross again.
You're becoming a regular. Can't say I mind the company.
Back for more, eh? I admire your persistence.
Ah, the traveler returns. Pull up a seat.
I thought I might see you again. How have you been?
Still wandering these parts? You'll be one of us before long.
Good to see you again. Truly.
The familiar face returns. What's on your mind this time?
Well, look who's back. I was just thinking about you.
Welcome back. You're starting to feel like a local.
Twice now. People will start to talk.
Ah, you again! Still in one piece, I see.
Back already? The road must not be offering much competition.
I had a feeling you'd return. Something about the way you left.
Well, if it isn't my favorite traveler. Welcome back.
You're becoming a fixture around here. Not that I'm complaining.
Returned, have you? Good. I've got more to say.
I see the road brought you back to us. Or was it something else?
Welcome back, friend. It's been quieter without you.
There you are. I was starting to wonder when you'd turn up again.
Good timing. I was hoping you'd come back before the weather turned.
You again! Starting to think you like it here.
The prodigal traveler returns. What news do you bring?
Ah, a face I recognize. That's always a comfort.
I see you haven't forgotten about us. Good.
Back so soon? Not that I'm keeping track.
Welcome home. Or close enough to home, anyway.
I knew you'd be back. Call it intuition.
You've returned! Things have been dull since your last visit.
Well, you keep showing up. I suppose that means something.
I remember you. You're the one who actually listens.
Good to have you back. The walls were starting to close in.
I was telling someone about you just the other day. And here you are.
Returned for more? I've always got more.
Ah, there's a face that's seen the road and come back to tell about it.
You're a sight for tired eyes. Welcome back.
Didn't think I'd see you again this soon. But I'm glad I did.
You've come back. That either means good news or bad news.
Still kicking around these parts, I see. Good for you.
The road gives and the road takes. Today it gave you back to us.
I was beginning to think you'd moved on for good. Glad I was wrong.
Twice in my settlement. You must see something worth coming back for.
There you are. I've been saving a conversation for you.
Well, hello again. We left off somewhere interesting last time.
You look well. The road hasn't beaten you down yet.
I thought about our last talk after you left. Welcome back.
You keep coming back, and I keep being glad about it.
Another visit! At this rate, I'll have to give you a bed.
I see you've found your way back without getting lost. Impressive.
Good to see a known face. These days, that's a luxury.
Welcome back. I've got fresh gossip, if you're interested.
Returning traveler! You must have heard I had something to share.
I remember our last conversation. I suspect you do too.
Ah, the wanderer returns. Sit. Stay. Tell me things.
I've been keeping an eye out for you. Glad you showed.
Your timing is something else. I was just thinking about what you said last time.
You came back. Not everyone does. I appreciate it.
Still here, still talking. Glad you're still listening.
The fact that you returned says more than words could.
Welcome back. The settlement hasn't changed much, but I have thoughts.
You're becoming part of the landscape. In a good way.
Twice now. That's no accident. What's on your mind?
The gate guard mentioned you were coming. I made sure to be here.
I had a feeling today would be a good day. Seeing you confirms it.
You've returned in one piece. That's better than some manage.
Another visit, another conversation. I could get used to this.
Glad you made it back safely. The road's been worse than usual.
It's you again. The one person I don't mind seeing twice.
Welcome back, friend. Pull up something to sit on and let's talk.
I hoped you'd return. There are things I didn't say last time.
You're beginning to wear a groove in that road. Not that I mind.
Familiar face, fresh visit. What can I do for you?
You've come back at an interesting moment. More on that in a minute.
I was sorting through my thoughts when you appeared. Perfect timing.
Still making the rounds? Good. There's always more to learn.
You look like you've been busy since we last spoke.
I've thought of a dozen things to tell you since your last visit.
Welcome back. You're one of the few people I look forward to seeing.
Returned, and looking none the worse for it. Good.
I see the world hasn't swallowed you up yet. Welcome back.
You're getting to know this place better than some who live here.
Another visit from the traveler. The settlement is better for it.
Well, you didn't forget about us out here. That counts for something.
Back again. I must be better company than I thought.
Glad you turned up. I've got an update on something we discussed.
The return of a welcome face. Sit down and tell me everything.
I was running out of people to talk to. Your timing is perfect.
You're starting to leave tracks around here. The familiar kind.
I half expected you. The other half is pleasantly surprised.
You came back. That tells me something about your character.
It's you. Good. There's something I wanted to follow up on.
The road must agree with you. You look well.
Here you are again. I was starting to think you'd forgotten the way.
Glad you're back. I have something that might interest you.
Another day, another visit from my favorite outsider.
I see you haven't tired of us yet. Good. Neither have I tired of you.
You're becoming a known quantity around here. That's a compliment.
Two visits make a pattern. I like this pattern.
I've been saving something for you. A piece of news, not a thing.
Welcome back. The place perks up when you're around.
I recognize those footsteps. Come in, come in.
You've returned, and just in time. Things have been developing.
I see you took the same road in. Smart. It's the safest.
Still curious about this place? Good. I'm still willing to talk.
Glad to see a traveler who keeps their word. You said you'd be back.
You again! I was just thinking about something you mentioned last time.
The road brought you back. I like to think the conversation helped.
Welcome home, stranger. Or should I stop calling you that?
You again. Good.
Ah, hello.
Well, look who it is.
Back, are you? Fine by me.
Oh, it's you. Come on in.
Thought I heard familiar footsteps.
You're back. Things have been quiet since you left.
Still in one piece. That's always nice to see.
I was wondering when you'd turn up again.
You've been gone a while. Everything all right?
The road keeps spitting you back this way. I don't mind.
Haven't changed a bit, have you? That's a good thing.
There's a face I know. Come, sit.
I see you remembered how to find us.
You look like the road's been kind to you. Good.
I was hoping you'd stop by again.
Well, you're persistent. I respect that.
Didn't think you'd be back this soon, but I'm not complaining.
The same road, the same face. Some things I don't mind repeating.
So you've come around again. What's different this time?
You're back. I had a few things I wanted to say, actually.
I noticed you coming up the path. Hard to miss someone you recognize.
Took your time getting back here, didn't you?
Oh. You. Good, I was getting bored.
The settlement's still standing, in case you were worried.
You look a little road-worn. Come rest a minute.
Same face, different day. I'll take it.
You know, I actually thought about you the other day.
Glad the road didn't swallow you whole.
Welcome back. Nothing's exploded since you left.
I had a feeling I'd see you before the week was out.
You're starting to know these paths better than some of us.
You again? This is becoming a habit. A good one.
Back from wherever you've been. I won't pry.
Still walking these roads? You've got stamina, I'll give you that.
Ah, the one who comes and goes. Mostly comes, lately.
Sit down. I was just talking and nobody was listening.
Well, you didn't vanish off the face of the world. Glad about that.
The familiar wanderer. I was starting to think of a nickname for you.
I notice you always come back at odd hours. Is that on purpose?
You look rested. Or at least more rested than last time.
Here we go again. That sounded negative, but I meant it warmly.
The dogs didn't even bark at you this time. You're basically a local.
I see you've been out doing things. I've been here doing nothing. Welcome back.
You picked a decent day to come back. Yesterday was miserable.
So you found your way back. I wasn't sure the road markings were still readable.
Every time you show up, I think of something I forgot to tell you last time.
You're becoming hard to surprise. You just keep showing up.
Good to see you haven't gotten yourself into too much trouble.
I owe you a proper hello from last time. Here it is: hello.
Well, the world didn't keep you. Our gain.
You've been gone just long enough for me to miss the company.
I'd pretend to be surprised, but we both know I was expecting you.
You've got new dust on your boots. Where have you been?
I see the road hasn't dulled that look in your eye.
Back for another round of whatever this is? Good.
You came back. I was about fifty-fifty on whether you would.
Something brought you back. Curiosity or stubbornness, I'd wager.
Oh, there you are. I was just getting used to the silence.
You look like you've been somewhere interesting since we last spoke.
I just told someone you'd probably be back soon. And here you are.
You keep coming back, so either I'm charming or you've got nowhere else to go.
I see the world treated you fair. Or at least didn't treat you too rough.
Still breathing, still walking. That's all I ask of people around here.
The last time you left, I thought of three things I should've said. Want to hear them?
You've been away. I can tell because it was quieter.
You're starting to feel less like a visitor and more like a neighbor.
Well, you made it back before the weather turned. Smart.
I kept your spot warm. Not literally, but the thought was there.
Hmm, you again. I suppose I should stop being surprised.
I was mid-thought when you appeared. Let me finish it. All right, done. Welcome back.
I notice you come back a little different each time. The road does that.
You look like you've got news. Or questions. Possibly both.
The gate guard waved you right through this time, I bet.
So the road loops back here again. I see the pattern.
I've been talking to the same five people for days. You're a welcome change.
You showed up just as I was running out of patience with everything. Good timing.
Some folks pass through once and never come back. You're not that kind.
I see you walking up the path and I think: at least it's not a stranger this time.
Still haven't had enough of this place? I understand: it grows on you.
You've got that look of someone who's been doing things. Tell me later.
Oh, you're back. Sit down before you fall down.
I notice you always come from the same direction. Creature of habit?
Welcome back. I reorganized while you were gone. Can't tell? Good.
I'm glad to see you, but I'm too tired to make a big thing of it.
You keep turning up, and I keep being fine with it.
Been a while. Or has it? Time moves strangely out here.
I remembered your face before I remembered your name. That's normal, right?
You don't look any worse than when you left. I'll call that a win.
Ah, there's the one who actually listens when I talk. Welcome back.
The settlement's not going anywhere, and neither am I. Glad you came back.
You're back, and I've got nothing new to report. But let's talk anyway.
I saw you from the window. Almost didn't recognize you with all that road dust.
You came back. I wasn't keeping count, but that makes this the nicest part of my week.
Oh good, someone who knows where the door is. Come in.
I had something to tell you but I've forgotten it. It'll come back. Welcome.
You know the routine by now. Sit, talk, ignore the mess.
The more I see you, the less I think of you as a stranger. That's progress.
You again. I'm running out of ways to say welcome back.
You look like you've been sleeping under the stars. I can tell by the neck.
I was just complaining that nobody interesting ever visits. And then you show up.
Good, you're here. I needed a second opinion on something.
You walk in like you own the place. I'm starting to think you do.
It's been too long. Or not long enough. Depends on who you ask.
I'd say you're one of us at this point, but the locals would argue.
You're back, and you brought that same curious look with you.
Still wandering, still ending up here. The road has a sense of humor.
I knew you'd be back when I saw the weather clearing up.
You return like the tide. Predictable but welcome.
Let me save you the trouble: yes, everything's the same as last time.
I see you found us again without getting lost. Improvement.
You've been gone long enough that I had to reintroduce myself in my head. Welcome back.
Still making the trip out here? You've got dedication.
The place hasn't fallen apart since you left. Small victories.
You look like you've got stories. But first, sit.
Ah, the traveler who actually comes back. That puts you in rare company.
You know, most people leave here and that's the end of it. You're different.
Every time you show up, I remember I actually enjoy talking to people.
Welcome back. I'd ask how you've been, but your boots tell the whole story.
You're here more often than the mail carrier. And more welcome, honestly.
I notice you always look around when you arrive. Checking if things changed?
The door was open because I had a feeling. There you go.
I see you haven't gotten any taller since last time. Neither have I.
You came back before I ran out of things to say. Just barely.
I was starting to think the road finally won. But here you are.
Good. You. I was about to have a very boring afternoon.
I won't ask where you've been. I'll just be glad you're here.
There's the face I was hoping to see today.
You look like you've walked a hundred miles since we last spoke. Have you?
I'm not going to make a fuss. Just know I'm glad you're back.
You've got a way of showing up at the right moment. Don't lose that.
Same settlement, same me, different visit. Let's make this one count.
I've been holding onto a thought for you. Sit down and I'll share it.
You came back. I won't pretend I'm not pleased.
The road keeps looping you back here. I'm choosing to take that as a compliment.
Still alive, still curious. Two of my favorite qualities in a person.
I was just about to do nothing. Now I'll do nothing with company. Welcome back.
You look good. Or at least better than the last time. The bar was low.
Funny how a familiar face changes the whole shape of a day.
I'm getting predictable: every time you show up, I stop what I'm doing.
You know the way to the good seat by now. Go ahead.
The fact that you keep coming back tells me more than any introduction ever did.
Well, here we are again. I'd have it no other way.
You're back. I had a whole speech prepared, but I forgot it. Welcome.
Oh, hello. Didn't hear you come in this time.
I told the others you'd be back. They didn't believe me.
You look different somehow. Can't place it. Anyway, welcome back.
The road gave you back and I'm not asking questions.
Back again. At this point you should just move in.
I see the dust on your coat. Must have been an interesting trip.
You know, the chair you sat in last time is still there. Go ahead.
Every time you leave, I think of better things I could have said.
Well, you survived. That's never a given around here.
Still coming back to this old corner of the world. I'm touched, honestly.
The weather's changed three times since you were last here. You haven't.
I was hoping you'd show up before I had to eat dinner alone again.
You look like you've learned a thing or two since we last spoke.
Same path, same door, same me. The only variable is you.
I can tell you've been busy. You've got that look.
I'm out of new things to say, but I'm never out of things to say. Welcome back.
You keep coming around and I keep having opinions. Works for both of us.
I see the road hasn't knocked the curiosity out of you.
Back already. I was just getting used to the peace and quiet.
You know, you're one of the few people who actually comes back on purpose.
I'd be lying if I said I didn't notice when you're gone.
You came back, and the timing couldn't be better.
Here you are. I was just running out of excuses not to work.
Still on your feet, still walking my way. I appreciate the consistency.
You again. I was starting to wonder if I'd dreamed the last visit.
The chickens recognized you before I did. Says something about my eyesight.
I notice you always arrive looking like you've got something to say. Go ahead.
You're back. Good. I've been arguing with myself and need a tiebreaker.
Welcome back. Try not to trip on the same loose board as last time.
You've been gone just the right amount of time. Any longer and I'd have worried.
I'd ask where you went, but I can smell the campfire from here.
There you are. The settlement feels a little fuller when you're around.
You look like the road agreed with you this time.
Back for more of my sparkling conversation? Can't blame you.
